summary: A Goodreads author profile for Thomas A. Edison, detailing his biographical background as a prolific inventor and businessman. The page highlights his pioneering work in mass production and industrial research, alongside community-contributed quotes reflecting his philosophies on failure, perseverance, and hard work.

principles.statement: Failure is not a lack of success, but a process of elimination.
principles.rationale: Every unsuccessful attempt is simply discovering a method that doesn't work, which is a necessary step toward finding the solution.
principles.source_quote: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.

principles.statement: Success often requires pushing through the urge to quit.
principles.rationale: People frequently give up without realizing they are on the brink of achieving their goal.
principles.source_quote: Many of life's failures are people who did not realize how close they were to success when they gave up.

signature_quotes.text: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.
Many of life's failures are people who did not realize how close they were to success when they gave up.
We often miss opportunity because it's dressed in overalls and looks like work

anti_patterns.description: Giving up in the face of repeated failure.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: You might be incredibly close to success without realizing it, making the act of quitting the actual cause of failure.

anti_patterns.description: Expecting opportunities to be easy or glamorous.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Opportunities are often disguised as hard labor ('dressed in overalls'), causing people to overlook them.
